price_list_2_date: Extract the price list issue date. Labels: 'DATE' / 'Date' / 'DATE PRICE LIST'. Example: 'DATE: 28 FEB, 2025'. DO NOT confuse with valid_till. Keep the exact format from the document and add to price_list_2_date.
producer: Identify the producer/manufacturer. Sources in priority order: (1) explicit 'PRODUCER' / 'MANUFACTURER' label; (2) 'Factory No' field paired with a producer name; (3) FALLBACK: when no explicit producer label exists, use the company name from the document HEADER. Keep the full name (and address if shown) in one line. Add to producer.
seller: Identify the seller. Labels: 'Seller' / 'Supplier'. Most price lists do NOT show an explicit 'Seller' label — in that case use the company name from the document HEADER. If neither is present, leave empty. Add to seller.
incoterms: Extract Incoterms — three-letter code and place. Labels: 'Term of shipment' / 'TERMS OF DELIVERY'. Examples: 'EXW RONGCHENG', 'FOB QINGDAO', 'CFR SAINT-PETERSBURG', 'DAP MOSCOW'. Keep wording exactly as printed. Add to incoterms.
valid_till: Extract validity period end. Labels: 'VALID TILL' / 'VALID UNTIL' / 'Valid until'. Example: 'VALID TILL: 15 APR, 2025'. DO NOT confuse with price_list_2_date. Keep the value as-is and add to valid_till.
HS_code: Extract HS/tariff code(s). Labels: 'HS CODE' / 'HS Code' / 'Tariff code'. Format may vary: 6 digits with dot ('0304.81'), 10 digits without separators ('0303899007'). If multiple, join with '; '. If no code, leave empty. Add to HS_code.
country_of_origin: Extract origin country. Labels: 'ORIGIN OF GOODS' / 'Country of origin' / 'Origin'. Examples: 'CHILE', 'CHINA'. If absent, leave empty. Add to country_of_origin.
products: The 'products' block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... for each line item in the price list, keeping order. For each position fill all available fields; if a shared attribute (e.g., one Latin species for multiple positions) is given once, duplicate it in the relevant product_i. In token_refs include references to the source fragments (cells/rows) used.
products[*].name_product: Extract the commercial name. Labels: 'PRODUCT' / 'Description' / 'DESCRIPTION OF PRODUCTS' / 'DESCRIPTION OF GOODS'. Join line breaks while keeping exact spelling. Add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Extract the Latin species name, typically in parentheses after the commercial name (e.g., '(LEPIDOCYBIUM FLAVOBRUNNEUM)'). Less commonly in a separate field 'LATIN NAME:'. Note: not every parenthesized text is Latin — pick only the scientific species name. If absent, leave empty. Add to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: Extract size/grade. Examples: '1-3KG', '3-6KG', '6-10KG', '10KG+', '0.8-1.2 KG', '3-4 LB (1.3-1.8 KG)', '80/120'. Keep both systems if both are printed. Add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Extract the pricing unit. For PRICE_LIST_2 the unit is typically 'KG' or 'KGS' (since pricing is per kilogram, distinguishing from PRICE_LIST_1 which prices per package). Labels: 'UNIT PRICE/KGS', 'KG'. Add to products[i].unit_box.
products[*].packages: Extract package quantity if a packaging column is present. Labels: 'Packages' / 'Cartons' / 'Cases'. Most price lists do NOT include package counts — if no such field, leave empty. Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].price_per_KG: Extract the unit price PER KILOGRAM. PRICE_LIST_2 specifically denotes pricing PER KILOGRAM (as opposed to PRICE_LIST_1 which is per package/unit). Labels: 'UNIT PRICE/KGS' / 'Price per KG' / 'Цена за кг'. Examples: 'RMB 24.61', 'RMB 28.22'. Keep currency and number format (dot/comma) exactly as in the document. Add to products[i].price_per_KG.
products[*].total_price: Extract line total if a total column exists. Labels: 'Total' / 'Line total'. Most price lists do NOT have line totals — if absent, leave empty (do not compute). Add to products[i].total_price.
